growth_records.medium: Optimized marine MTB medium (Silva et al. ref [21]) with succinate carbon source

up.units: n/a (only % remaining reported: 62% of inoculation concentration at 120 h)
up.method: HPLC/assay of medium succinate; consumed throughout growth

up.units: n/a (relative % tracked; greatest consumption 48-72 h; 3 pulses needed to restore initial)
n/a (~80% consumed in exponential phase; greatest 48-72 h)

growth_records.cite: Correa, Tarcisio et al. Continuous Production of Biogenic Magnetite Nanoparticles by the Marine Bacterium Magnetovibrio blakemorei Strain MV-1T with a Nitrous Oxide Injection Strategy. Marine Drugs (2022). DOI:10.3390/md20110724. [PMC9692579]
growth_records.snippet: growth was observed up to 120 h, with the highest specific growth rate observed between 24 h and 48 h (mu_max = 0.05 h-1... Maximum magnetite productivity was reached at 72 h (p = 1.65 mg/L/h or 39.7 mg/L/day)
growth_records.notes: Fed-batch record. mu_max=0.05 h-1 reported (Fig 1a, 24-48 h). Doubling time = ln2/mu = 0.693/0.05 = 13.86 h (calculated). Substrate uptakes (succinate, Fe2+, N2O, amino-N) are experimentally tracked but reported only as % of initial in figures, so no mmol/gDW/h derivable. Succinate at 120 h = 62% of inoculation value. Magnetite (product) production 32.5 mg/L at 72 h, productivity 39.7 mg/L/day. T=28 C, pH strictly 7.0+/-0.2 (auto NaOH/HCl). Anaerobic, N2O as terminal electron acceptor. Medium composition per ref [21], not restated here.

growth_records.medium: Optimized marine MTB medium (Silva et al. ref [21]) with succinate carbon source

up.units: n/a (kept constant at steady state; relative % tracked)

growth_records.cite: Correa, Tarcisio et al. Continuous Production of Biogenic Magnetite Nanoparticles by the Marine Bacterium Magnetovibrio blakemorei Strain MV-1T with a Nitrous Oxide Injection Strategy. Marine Drugs (2022). DOI:10.3390/md20110724. [PMC9692579]
growth_records.snippet: We only employed one dilution rate (D = 0.035 h-1), which was equivalent to 70% of the growth rate in the exponential phase (24-72 h)... magnetite production and productivity were 27.1 mg/L and 22.7 mg/L/day, respectively, at 120 h
growth_records.notes: Chemostat record. D=0.035 h-1 reported (= 70% of exponential mu_max, consistent with mu_max~0.05 h-1). At steady state mu=D=0.035 h-1; doubling = ln2/0.035 = 19.8 h (calculated). Magnetite (product) 27.1 mg/L, productivity 22.7 mg/L/day at 120 h. Substrate consumption (succinate, Fe2+, amino-N) held constant at steady state but only relative % reported (Fig 3b); no mmol/gDW/h. T=28 C, pH 7.0, anaerobic with N2O.

phenotypes.base_medium: Sole-substrate growth / assimilation on a defined minimal medium (substrate as C/N/energy source) — positive = the organism grows on it
phenotypes.citation: BacDive strain 23250 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/23250
